name: Vegetable Salad with Romesco Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Green beans, cut in halves
1 medium Zucchini, cut into 1/4-inch slices, each slice cut in half
1 small Cucumber, cut into 1/4-inch slices, each slice cut in half
14 Green olives stuffed with Pimento, chopped finely
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. To prepare the romesco sauce: Put the tomato and garlic into a small baking pan and roast in a preheated 400-degree oven for 20-25 minutes, until tender and starting to brown.
2. Heat 3 tablespoons olive oil in a small skillet on medium heat. Add the bread crumbs and almonds, cooking until golden.
3. Puree the roasted tomato and garlic in a food processor. Add the bread crumb mixture, processing until the almonds are very finely chopped. Add the crushed pepper, salt, 5 tablespoons vinegar, and the remaining 3 tablespoons olive oil. Process until thickened.
4. To prepare the salad: Cut the potato into half, place in a saucepan, cover with water, and cook until tender for about 20-25 minutes. Drain; peel and cut into thin slices.
5. Cover the onion with cold water and set aside for 10 minutes. Drain and pat dry. Bring a pan of water to boil, add the beans, and cook for 5 minutes until tender. Remove the beans with a slotted spoon to a bowl of ice water. Bring the water back to boil, add the zucchini, and cook for 1 minute. Add to the beans in the ice water when chilled. Drain and pat dry.
6. Combine the potatoes, onions, beans, zucchini, cucumber, olives, pimento, egg, several grindings of pepper, parsley with the dressing. Let sit at room temperature or refrigerate for longer storage. Stir in the remaining tablespoon vinegar if needed.
